basics.name: Andri Óskarsson
basics.label: Computer Engineer - Software & Infrastructure
basics.summary: 28 years of building software and the infrastructure it runs on. From managing 50 servers and Cisco networks to shipping React apps and Go services.

work.summary:
Responsible for infrastructure, developer experience and leading the frontend architecture for a SaaS for Danish craftsmen.
Consolidated our control-panel from two solutions into a singular React/TypeScript application enabling new feature development while maintaining existing functionality.
Set up Gitops (trunk-based deployment), improved developer tooling and VPN. Migrated our hosting from AWS to a bare-metal Kubernetes (k3s) cluster on Hetzner with significant cost-savings and better performance.
Most recently supercharged our review and issue triaging with help of agentic coding.
Helped a customer in-house for 7 months with improving their frontend code and toolchain. Consulted and helped plan customer-projects.

Assembled a small team and delivered a resilient, offline capable point-of-sale application in React Native using event-sourcing and SQLite. Built our backend in TypeScript/Node and GraphQL. Set up a managed Kubernetes cluster and Postgres database at GCP. Automated Gitops pipelines from commit to production.

Helped to develop a teaching platform for Danish education publisher in React/TypeScript. Developed a mobile app to help librarians work more effectively in React Native. Pitched and developed an image (and video) scaling micro service in Go.

Pitched, developed and delivered a new user-focused app in React Native, bumping it from 2 stars to 4 on app-stores. Migrated our LAMP stack from a single server to managed Kubernetes on GCP. Ran in-house development, trained new employees, managed our infrastructure, coordinated with freelancers and did full-stack software development.

I managed releases, did frontend development and was a Scrum-Master on "Min Uddannelse", a large learning platform. I helped introduce React to the organisation, did internal training and helped with recruiting.
Worked on an internal billing and CRM system as a part of the backend team. I pitched and delivered an automatic plan-upgrade system for usage-warnings that was generating revenue.
Worked on automatic sorting machines for a large, American clothing store.
Worked closely with one of our customers on creating a shipping calculator for their website, and a reverse-engineered thermal printer solution.
Developed an Android app and a backend that used geolocation to provide the users with location-aware ads.

Built and managed our local server-room of 50 physical Linux servers on-premises. Set up our network along with VLANs and fiber-optic Internet connection using Cisco and Linux. Researched and deployed a fully functional VoIP telephone system using Asterisk with physical and software clients. Supported the development team with testing environments, source-control and workstations.
Managed servers, routers and switches. Customer support.
Created custom CMS systems for cinemas and real-estate brokers.
Installed and supported our B2B customers with DSL and fiber connections. Did some minor system administration as well.
Custom PHP solutions for our customers.
Tested releases, collected binaries on the Internet for a false-positive database. Did some minor web development.
